title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After searching a dead man's pockets, Anthony discovers a scrap of paper containing the text: "Eleven forty-five, Thursday, Chimney House." Virginia Rue notes that she was scheduled to visit Chimney House that night. She mentions that her cousin, George Romex, does not want her to go, though she doubts he would commit murder. Anthony suggests that Virginia proceed with her plans to confuse potential conspirators and offers to handle the situation.

To facilitate the plan, Virginia sends her maid, Alice, away on a false errand. Anthony selects a large suitcase from the basement to dispose of the body. He instructs Virginia to take her luggage and the suitcase to Paddington Station, where she is to drop a luggage ticket for him to retrieve. Virginia expresses gratitude, and when Anthony mentions his friend Jimmy McGregor, Virginia reacts with surprise, suggesting she knows him.

Anthony executes the plan by retrieving the suitcase, placing Giuseppe's body in it, and driving to the outskirts of London. He obscures his car's license plate with mud, leaves the body by a roadside bend, and hides a gun in a tree crevice. He then stores the suitcase in a cloakroom at Paddington Station before driving to Chimney House.

Arriving at Chimney House at 11:30 PM, Anthony scales the wall. At 11:45 PM, he hears a gunshot coming from inside the house. After investigating the locked windows, he sees a light flicker briefly in a second-floor room before the building returns to darkness. The following morning, Inspector Bagshaw receives a phone call from "Your Lordship" that leaves him visibly affected.
